name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 06.07.2026

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.07.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 06.07.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Kharif Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 05 July 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 350.85 lakh ha (including 57.58 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 442.80 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Kharif  sowing  data  collection  has  started  from  05 th June,  2026  and  the  sowing  is  in  preliminary stage. As on 05 July 2026, the area sown under Kharif crops is 350.85 lakh ha (including 57.58 lakh ha in Sugarcane) as compared to 442.80 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Assam  and  Meghalaya, Arunachal  Pradesh,  at  isolated  places  over  Bihar,  East  UP,  East  Rajasthan  and  Tamil  Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in  Assam  and  Meghalaya, Arunachal  Pradesh,  at  isolated  places  over  Bihar,  East  UP,  East  Rajasthan  and  Tamil  Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  01  June  to  01 July  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  38% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: -30%; (iii) Central India: -45%; and South Peninsula: -26%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  01  June  to  01 July  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  38% lower than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: (-) 40%; (ii) Northwest India: -30%; (iii) Central India: -45%; and South Peninsula: -26%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Very heavy to extremely Heavy rainfall likely over isolated place in Chattisgarh, Gujarat, MP, Maharashtra and Konkan Region till 7 th July 2026.
texts.text:  Very heavy to extremely Heavy rainfall likely over isolated place in Chattisgarh, Gujarat, MP, Maharashtra and Konkan Region till 7 th July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  East  Rajasthan,  Himachal  Pradesh,  Jharkhand,  Karnataka, Kerala,  MP,  Odisha,  Uttarakhand,  East  Uttar  Pradesh  and  Sub  Himalayan  West  Bengal  & Sikkim.
texts.text:  Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  over  East  Rajasthan,  Himachal  Pradesh,  Jharkhand,  Karnataka, Kerala,  MP,  Odisha,  Uttarakhand,  East  Uttar  Pradesh  and  Sub  Himalayan  West  Bengal  & Sikkim.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Thunderstorm over isolated places at Rajasthan, J&K, Ladakh, MP from 6-11 th July 2026 and at Gujrat, Maharashtra, Keral, TN and Karnataka on 6-7 th July 2026.
texts.text:  Thunderstorm over isolated places at Rajasthan, J&K, Ladakh, MP from 6-11 th July 2026 and at Gujrat, Maharashtra, Keral, TN and Karnataka on 6-7 th July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Hot and Humid weather at isolated places over Assam & Meghalaya till 6 th July 2026.
texts.text:  Hot and Humid weather at isolated places over Assam & Meghalaya till 6 th July 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  2  July  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 47.725 billion cubic meters, which is 26.00% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 71.20 percent of last  year's  storage  and  98.60  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  2  July  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 47.725 billion cubic meters, which is 26.00% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 71.20 percent of last  year's  storage  and  98.60  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is less than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole but is better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels: The groundwater situation is largely stable across the country. Out of 9,085 monitoring wells,  87.8%  (7,977  wells)  fall  under  the  normal  category,  while  12.2%  of  the  wells exhibit varying levels of groundwater drought.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels: The groundwater situation is largely stable across the country. Out of 9,085 monitoring wells,  87.8%  (7,977  wells)  fall  under  the  normal  category,  while  12.2%  of  the  wells exhibit varying levels of groundwater drought.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Approximately  69%  of  the  wells  have  groundwater  levels  within  10  metres  below  ground  level, whereas  only  about  4%  have  groundwater  levels  deeper  than  40  metres, indicating  good groundwater availability across most regions.
texts.text: Approximately  69%  of  the  wells  have  groundwater  levels  within  10  metres  below  ground  level, whereas  only  about  4%  have  groundwater  levels  deeper  than  40  metres, indicating  good groundwater availability across most regions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Groundwater  drought  is  predominantly  mild  and  confined  to  limited  areas.  Among  the  affected wells,  7.2%  are  classified  as  experiencing  mild  drought,  2.2%  moderate  drought,  0.9%  severe drought, and 1.9% extreme drought.
texts.text: Groundwater  drought  is  predominantly  mild  and  confined  to  limited  areas.  Among  the  affected wells,  7.2%  are  classified  as  experiencing  mild  drought,  2.2%  moderate  drought,  0.9%  severe drought, and 1.9% extreme drought.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: text
texts.orig: According to the Pre-Monsoon 2026 Groundwater Drought Index, groundwater conditions across the country are predominantly normal, with groundwater stress restricted to limited areas in a few states.
texts.text: According to the Pre-Monsoon 2026 Groundwater Drought Index, groundwater conditions across the country are predominantly normal, with groundwater stress restricted to limited areas in a few states.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases :  Over all intensity of pests and disease remained below Economic Threshold Level (ETL).
texts.text: Pests & Diseases :  Over all intensity of pests and disease remained below Economic Threshold Level (ETL).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During  the  week  (25 th June  -  1 st July  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 28%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 1st July 2026) is lower than LPA by 38%.
texts.text:  During  the  week  (25 th June  -  1 st July  2026),  lower  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 28%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01st June to 1st July 2026) is lower than LPA by 38%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country except North East India, where it is likely to be below normal during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country except North East India, where it is likely to be below normal during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be near normal over the country as whole. It is likely to be above normal over northwest and central India and below normal over northeast and south peninsular India during the week.
texts.text:  Overall, rainfall is likely to be near normal over the country as whole. It is likely to be above normal over northwest and central India and below normal over northeast and south peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (02 July - 08 July 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (02 July - 08 July 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant changes in Maximum temperature over the country till 08 July 2026.
texts.text:  No significant changes in Maximum temperature over the country till 08 July 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Maximum temperatures  likely  to  rise  by  1.6-3°C  over  some  parts  of  northeast  India  and  isolated pockets of Northwest India
texts.text:  Maximum temperatures  likely  to  rise  by  1.6-3°C  over  some  parts  of  northeast  India  and  isolated pockets of Northwest India

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (09 July - 15 July 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (09 July - 15 July 2026):

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant Heatwave conditions are likely over any part of the country during the week.
texts.text:  No significant Heatwave conditions are likely over any part of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of the plains of northeast  India.  Near-normal  weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during week 2.
texts.text:  Above normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of the plains of northeast  India.  Near-normal  weekly maximum temperatures (-1.5 to +1.5°C) are likely across the rest of the country during week 2.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No  Warm  Night  conditions  are  likely  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the  week.  However, minimum temperatures are likely to be near normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) over parts of northwest India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, & Uttar Pradesh), and near Normal (-1.5 to +1.5°C) for the country.
texts.text:  No  Warm  Night  conditions  are  likely  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the  week.  However, minimum temperatures are likely to be near normal (+1.6 to +3.0°C) over parts of northwest India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, & Uttar Pradesh), and near Normal (-1.5 to +1.5°C) for the country.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  22°N/60°E,  22°N/65°E,  Jamnagar, Udaipur, Ajmer, Jhunjhunu, Hisar, Bhatinda and 32.5°N/70°E.
texts.text:  The  Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  22°N/60°E,  22°N/65°E,  Jamnagar, Udaipur, Ajmer, Jhunjhunu, Hisar, Bhatinda and 32.5°N/70°E.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of North Arabian  Sea,  Gujarat,  remaining  parts  of  Haryana  &  Punjab,  and  some  more  parts  of  Rajasthan during next 3 days.
texts.text:  Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of North Arabian  Sea,  Gujarat,  remaining  parts  of  Haryana  &  Punjab,  and  some  more  parts  of  Rajasthan during next 3 days.

texts.orig: As on 02 July, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 47.725 BCM (26.00%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 02 July, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 47.725 BCM (26.00%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Soil moisture during 03 July 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major parts of Western MP, Parts of Maharashtra, Northern pockets of U.P, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, H.P and J&K.
texts.text:  Soil moisture during 03 July 2026 is similar to the average of the past 10 years in major parts of Western MP, Parts of Maharashtra, Northern pockets of U.P, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, H.P and J&K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Jharkhand, Haryana, Bihar, Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Marathwada  region  of  Maharashtra,    Eastern  part  of    Madhya  Pradesh, Eastern  &  Bundelkhand  region  of  UP,  North  Karnataka,  Telangana,  Southern  W.B    & Northeastern part of India.
texts.text:  It was lower than average of past 10 years in Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Jharkhand, Haryana, Bihar, Rajasthan,  Gujarat,  Marathwada  region  of  Maharashtra,    Eastern  part  of    Madhya  Pradesh, Eastern  &  Bundelkhand  region  of  UP,  North  Karnataka,  Telangana,  Southern  W.B    & Northeastern part of India.
